title: Compatibility-consumer inventory and deletion allowlist

allowlist.coverage: Each scoped consumer names owner, operation, thin projection fields, rich compatibility fields, deferred fields, and retirement checkpoint.

post_todo5_reconciliation.reason: Todo 5 moved scoped UI consumer lines after the original inventory was checked in.
post_todo5_reconciliation.action: Expanded the checked-in inventory with six Todo 5 UI read-model consumers and regenerated source-line reference anchors; no product behavior or plan changed.

verification.result: PASS; immutable archive checksum/mode, sidecars, redaction, and packet index verified

risk_probes.dirty_worktree: DETECTED pre-existing edits in mission read-model files plus this task's untracked files; no unrelated files modified by this task.
risk_probes.stale_state: PASS; scanner reports stale=[] and repeated checks are deterministic.
risk_probes.misleading_success_output: PASS; failure-fixture probe requires and observes a non-empty unknown result.
risk_probes.flaky_tests: PASS; targeted pytest passed in three consecutive runs.
risk_probes.other_classes: Not applicable to this static inventory boundary: network, database corruption, browser rendering, and concurrency races.

manual_qa.observed: JSON output showed 18 target files and 275 scoped references with no unknown or stale keys.

packet_reconciliation.index_and_readme: Updated to distinguish the historical 12/251 pre-Todo-5 baseline from the authoritative 18/275 post-Todo-5 inventory.

cleanup_receipt.temporary_baseline_file: not retained as a product artifact

risks: The broad baseline includes unrelated consumers outside the scoped M6 surfaces; those remain intentionally outside this deletion allowlist.
The 12-file/251-reference values are historical pre-Todo-5 baseline evidence only; 18 files/275 references is the authoritative post-Todo-5 inventory.
Line-key references require an allowlist update when a scoped consumer moves lines; this is deliberate fail-closed behavior before deletion checkpoints.
